sections.paragraph:
Βαρναβᾶς, ᾶ, ὁ (Βαρνάβας edd.; ברנבו? SEG VII, 381, 5.—See Dssm., B 175ff, NB 16 [BS 187ff, 307ff], ZNW 7, 1906, 91f; Dalman, Worte 32, Gram.2 178, 4; HCadbury, Semitic Personal Names in Luke-Acts: RHarris Festschr. [Amicitiae Corolla], ed. HWood ’33, 47f, JBL 52, ’33, 59) Barnabas, a Levite fr. Cyprus, whose first name was Joseph (Ac 4:36), uncle of John Mark Col 4:10 (cp. Ac 15:37) and freq. cited in association w. Paul Ac 9:27; 11:22, 30; 12:25; chs. 13–15 (18 times); 1 Cor 9:6; Gal 2:1, 9, 13; Col 4:10; 2 Cor subscr.; B subscr. In Ac 4:36 his name is translated υἱὸς παρακλήσεως son of consolation, but it is not quite clear how this rendering, prob. a popular etymology, is derived.—RTaylor, CQR 136, ’43, 59–79; Bruce, Acts 160.—M-M.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

Amicitiae Amicitiae Corolla = JRHarris Festschrift—List 6

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

  1. fr. = from

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

subscr. subscr. = subscription

prob. prob. = probable, probably

CQR CQR = Church Quarterly Review—List 6

Bruce, Acts Bruce, Acts = FB., The Acts of the Apostles: Greek Text w. Introduction and Commentary—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 167
